Politics.headline: US and Israel launch attack on Iran, explosions in Israel, Gulf Arab states
Politics.detail: The US and Israel have launched a military attack on Iran, with explosions reported in Israel and several Gulf Arab states, escalating regional tensions.

Politics.headline: Trump orders federal agencies to stop using Anthropic technology amid dispute over ethics of AI
Politics.detail: President Trump has ordered federal agencies to discontinue using AI technology from Anthropic due to an ongoing dispute regarding the ethics of artificial intelligence.

Politics.headline: Green Party wins UK by-election in blow to Labour, PM Starmer
Politics.detail: The Green Party secured a victory in a UK by-election, dealing a setback to the Labour Party and Prime Minister Starmer, as the progressive Greens won in a former Labour stronghold.

Politics.headline: Pakistan says it is in 'open war' with Afghanistan as nations exchange strikes
Politics.detail: Pakistan has declared that it is in an 'open war' with Afghanistan amidst the exchange of strikes between the two nations.

Politics.headline: Democratic Alliance leader addresses Eastern Cape Provincial Congress
Politics.detail: John Steenhuisen, Leader of the Democratic Alliance, addressed the Eastern Cape Provincial Congress in Gqeberha, emphasizing the need for change and governance focused on action rather than words.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Stocks Drop After Trump Ramps Up His Tariffs and Investors Dump Potential AI Losers
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks sank as Wall Street kept punishing companies that could become losers in the AI revolution and after President Trump increased tariffs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Germany Inflation Slows to 1.9% in February
Finance & Economy.detail: Germany's annual inflation rate eased to 1.9% in February 2026 from 2.1% in January, coming in slightly below market expectations of 2.0%, according to preliminary estimates.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump orders federal agencies to stop using Anthropic as dispute escalates
Finance & Economy.detail: There has been a weeks-long feud between the Pentagon and Anthropic over concerns about how the military could use AI at war, leading President Trump to order federal agencies to discontinue using Anthropic.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK withdraws staff from Iran amid threat of US strikes
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK Foreign Office has 'temporarily' withdrawn its staff from Iran amid the threat of US strikes against the country.

Finance & Economy.headline: New U.S. tariffs take effect today after Supreme Court ruling
Finance & Economy.detail: New US tariffs take effect today following a Supreme Court ruling, impacting markets and prompting review by the Commerce Ministry.

Technology.headline: Trump Orders Federal Agencies to Stop Using Anthropic AI, Cites Safety Concerns
Technology.detail: President Trump has directed all federal agencies to immediately cease using AI technology from Anthropic due to the company's refusal to grant the Pentagon unfettered access and concerns over its potential use in mass surveillance and autonomous weapons. Anthropic's CEO, Dario Amodei, had insisted on 'red lines' regarding the use of AI for military purposes, leading to the dispute.

Technology.headline: Apple's AI Wearables and Product Week Could Reshape Long-Term Growth
Technology.detail: Apple is reportedly accelerating work on AI-integrated wearables, including smart glasses and AI pendants, with launches expected in 2026, and CEO Tim Cook has hinted at an upcoming product week featuring a low-cost MacBook and a foldable iPhone. This push into AI hardware and a broader hardware refresh could broaden Apple's reach beyond its core devices.

Technology.headline: Block Cuts 40% of Staff as It Doubles Down on AI Tools and Bitcoin Bets
Technology.detail: Block, the parent company of Square, plans to lay off approximately 40% of its workforce as it pivots towards artificial intelligence tools. The company is also maintaining its investment in Bitcoin, which contributed to a quarterly loss due to the cryptocurrency's decline.

Technology.headline: Google Rolls Out Nano Banana 2, Borrows 2 Notable Features From Pro Model
Technology.detail: Google has launched Nano Banana 2, its latest AI image generation model, incorporating enhanced features from the Pro version. This update brings improved capabilities to a wider audience.

Technology.headline: Prime Minister Gains New Insight into Use of Drones and Emerging Technologies in Ukraine
Technology.detail: Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre of Norway visited Kyiv to gain firsthand insight into the use of drones and emerging technologies in the war, noting the rapid technological developments resulting from cooperation between Norwegian and Ukrainian companies. Norway plans to provide significant support to Ukraine in 2026 for the procurement of drones and autonomous systems.

Science.headline: Scientists Discover Microbe That Breaks a Fundamental Rule of the Genetic Code
Science.detail: Researchers at UC Berkeley have discovered a methane-producing archaeon that sometimes reads a specific three-letter DNA code as a green light instead of a stop signal, adding an unusual amino acid and continuing to build the protein, resulting in two different proteins that can emerge from the same code.

Science.headline: NASA scraps its 2027 moon landing, adds two missions in 2028
Science.detail: NASA is revamping its Artemis moon landing program, and the Artemis III mission will now focus on docking and space suit tests in low Earth orbit instead of landing astronauts on the moon. NASA's administrator announced the shake-up in the flight lineup after the space agency's new moon rocket returned to its Florida hangar for more repairs.

Science.headline: National Science Day: India's roadmap to become superpower by 2035
Science.detail: India is observing National Science Day on February 28, marking the discovery of the Raman Effect by physicist Sir CV Raman and highlighting the country's progress towards becoming a global superpower through advancements in space exploration, defense, and innovation, including missions like Gaganyaan and Chandrayaan-4.

Science.headline: Biodegradable Packaging Film Made Partly from Milk Protein
Science.detail: Scientists have created a new contender to tackle plastic pollution: a biodegradable packaging film made partly from milk protein. Researchers at Flinders University blended calcium caseinate with starch and natural nanoclay to create the film.

Science.headline: Handwriting activates neural networks that keyboards miss
Science.detail: A revived Swiss fountain pen brand and new brain science converge on the same thesis: handwriting activates neural networks that keyboards miss. Handwriting forces the production of variable, imperfect letterforms — what neuroscientists call "noisy" motor outputs which compels the brain to abstract the essential features of each character, recruiting the reading circuit.

Health.headline: PM Modi to launch nationwide HPV vaccination drive on February 28
Health.detail: India will use Gardasil, a quadrivalent HPV vaccine, for protection from HPV types 16. The nationwide HPV vaccination drive is scheduled to be launched by PM Modi on February 28.

Health.headline: 'Viruses don't know borders': US anti-vaccine rhetoric could impact global measles crisis
Health.detail: The spread of anti-vaccine rhetoric in the U.S. could have global implications, potentially impacting efforts to control the measles crisis. Measles cases have been sharply increasing in the Americas, prompting warnings from the UN agency.

Health.headline: New measles case reported in Bryan County is the 2nd in Georgia this year
Health.detail: The Georgia Department of Public Health (DPH) has confirmed a measles case from an unvaccinated Bryan County resident, marking the second reported measles case in Georgia this year.

Health.headline: Spain alerts WHO of swine flu virus believed to have been transmitted between people
Health.detail: Spain has alerted the World Health Organization (WHO) about a swine flu virus that is believed to have been transmitted between people.

Health.headline: Trump's pesticide and vaccine moves spur MAHA backlash ahead of midterms
Health.detail: President Trump's health policies, intended to rally 'Make America Healthy Again' voters, face setbacks due to a measles outbreak and concerns over his vaccine agenda. An executive order protecting glyphosate-based herbicides, supported by HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has also drawn criticism.

Sports.headline: Kildare Hurling Team vs Down
Sports.detail: Kildare Hurling Team will face Down on Sunday at 12.45pm; commentary with Seamus Dillon in association with Hanlon Concrete, Robertstown.

Sports.headline: Kildare LGFA vs Meath
Sports.detail: Kildare LGFA will play against Meath on Saturday at 5pm in Hawkfield.

Sports.headline: Kildare Football Team vs Meath
Sports.detail: Kildare Football Team will face Meath on Saturday at 7pm, with commentary by Ger McNally and Paul Cribbin, in association with Hanlon Concrete, Robertstown.

Sports.headline: LA Galaxy vs Charlotte FC | Feb. 28, 2026
Sports.detail: The LA Galaxy will return to MLS Regular Season play at Dignity Health Sports Park on Saturday, Feb. 28 against Charlotte FC.

Sports.headline: NRL Vegas 2026: Teams get first look inside Allegiant Stadium, Matt Burton almost hits roof, video, latest news
Sports.detail: NRL teams get their first look inside Allegiant Stadium in Las Vegas, with Matt Burton almost hitting the roof with a kick.

Entertainment.headline: Neil Sedaka, singer-songwriter of hits like 'Breaking Up Is Hard to Do,' dies at 86
Entertainment.detail: Neil Sedaka, the million-selling singer and songwriter known for hits like “Breaking Up Is Hard to Do,” “Calendar Girl,” and “Oh! Carol,” has died at the age of 86.

Entertainment.headline: Demi Moore goes viral looking unrecognizable with dramatic new bob haircut transformation
Entertainment.detail: Demi Moore's dramatic new bob haircut has the internet buzzing, with many fans expressing surprise at her transformed look.

Entertainment.headline: Skydance's 20-year rise to overtake Paramount, Warner Bros
Entertainment.detail: Skydance Productions, founded by David Ellison, is on the cusp of a takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery, marking a significant rise from its initial box office struggles.

Entertainment.headline: Thalapathy Vijay and Sangeeta Sornalingam's love story
Entertainment.detail: Tamil superstar Thalapathy Vijay's wife, Sangeeta Sornalingam, has filed for divorce in the Chengalpattu Family Court.

Entertainment.headline: The Kerala Story 2 box office prediction: Sequel to open at just half of part 1
Entertainment.detail: The Kerala Story 2 is predicted to have a decent opening at the box office, although it's expected to be only half of what the first film earned.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bill Clinton says he 'did nothing wrong' with Epstein as he faced grilling over their relationship
Crime & Justice.detail: Bill Clinton testified before the House Oversight Committee regarding his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, stating he "saw nothing that gave me pause" during their time together and that he would have reported Epstein if he had known about the alleged sex trafficking. The deposition marked the first time a former president has been compelled to testify to Congress, with Clinton emphasizing he did nothing wrong.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former imam found guilty of multiple rapes and sexual assaults
Crime & Justice.detail: Abdul Halim Khan, a former Imam, was convicted of twenty-one counts of rape, sexual assault, and child sexual offenses against seven women and girls in East London. The Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) prosecuted Khan.

Crime & Justice.headline: Florida Man Sentenced for Aggravated Identity Theft and Fraud Committed Against at Least 450 Victims
Crime & Justice.detail: A Florida man was sentenced for aggravated identity theft and fraud impacting at least 450 victims. The U.S. Department of Justice issued a press release regarding the sentencing.

Crime & Justice.headline: Opinion: A Black History Month call to reform New York's mental health courts
Crime & Justice.detail: As Black History Month closes, there's a call to remember the weaponization of mental illness against Black activists and to reform New York's mental health courts, with emphasis on the Treatment Court Expansion Act to dismantle racist systems criminalizing behavioral health diagnoses in Black communities. The Treatment Court Expansion Act would establish access to clinically appropriate, non-institutionalized treatment in every county of New York.

Crime & Justice.headline: Board members appointed to the Children and Family Court Advisory Support Service
Crime & Justice.detail: New board members have been appointed to the Children and Family Court Advisory Support Service. This announcement was made on February 27, 2026.

Education.headline: Schools in 'eye of the storm' of SEND complaints
Education.detail: Schools are facing increasing complaints related to Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND), highlighting the challenges in providing adequate support and resources.

Education.headline: Minister's rallying cry for expert SEND staff to return
Education.detail: A government minister is urging experienced Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) staff to return to the education sector to address the ongoing crisis.

Education.headline: 75 govt primary schools shut in two years in Gujarat
Education.detail: In Gujarat, 75 government primary schools have been shut down in the past two years, raising concerns about access to education in the region.

Education.headline: Several Chandigarh schools receive bomb threat emails; security tightened
Education.detail: Following bomb threat emails, security has been increased at several schools in Chandigarh to ensure the safety of students and staff.

Education.headline: ED Changes Timeline to Recognize New Accreditors
Education.detail: The Department of Education has altered the timeline for recognizing new accreditors, which could impact higher education institutions seeking accreditation.

Lifestyle.headline: Alia Bhatt Slays In An All-Black Gucci Ensemble At Milan Fashion Week
Lifestyle.detail: The Bollywood actress made a stunning appearance at Milan Fashion Week, showcasing an all-black Gucci outfit, highlighting the latest fashion trends.

Lifestyle.headline: Cheese sold at Walmart recalled in 24 states over potential health risk.
Lifestyle.detail: A cheese product sold at Walmart has been recalled in 24 states due to a potential health risk, prompting concerns among consumers.

Lifestyle.headline: Star Awards 2026: Emerald Hill leads nominations with 17 nods in 9 categories.
Lifestyle.detail: Emerald Hill leads the nominations for the Star Awards 2026 with 17 nominations across 9 categories, marking a significant achievement in the entertainment industry.

Lifestyle.headline: Shakira chose Rashmika Mandanna's wedding designer for a sultry concert costume.
Lifestyle.detail: Shakira collaborated with Rashmika Mandanna's wedding designer to create a sultry costume for a recent concert, showcasing a blend of fashion influences.

Lifestyle.headline: Researchers find TikTok fitness videos cause body image issues.
Lifestyle.detail: A recent study indicates that exposure to fitness videos on TikTok can contribute to negative body image perceptions, raising concerns about social media's impact on mental health.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Confronting an uncomfortable past
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Today marks the 79th anniversary of the 228 Incident, an anti-government protest that began after a Taiwanese widow was assaulted; official abuse escalated into a brutal crackdown by the Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) that left thousands dead.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Guest Editorial: SOTU lacked point
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The State of the Union address by President Trump represented a new low point and no longer serves a useful purpose in the political life of the nation, with the spectacle making the country worse off.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Morning Briefing: Feb. 28, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The briefing includes news such as deadly Afghan airstrikes that Kabul blames on Pakistan, President Donald Trump signaling a “big decision” on Iran amid conflicting reports, and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy addressing nuclear weapons claims from the UK and France.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A new Gallup poll shows how Americans' sympathies have shifted in the Israeli-Palestinian conflict
Opinion/Editorial.detail: American sympathies in the Middle East have shifted dramatically toward the Palestinians, according to new Gallup polling, after decades of overwhelming support for the Israelis; this shift accelerated during the war in Gaza.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trump raises the possibility of a 'friendly takeover of Cuba' coming out of talks with Havana
Opinion/Editorial.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday that the U.S. is in talks with Havana and raised the possibility of a “friendly takeover of Cuba” without offering any details on what he meant; Secretary of State Marco Rubio was in discussions with Cuban leaders “at a very high level.”

World.headline: US and Israel attack Iran: What we know so far
World.detail: The US and Israel have bombed multiple Iranian cities, including Tehran, prompting Iran to retaliate with missiles aimed at Israel and US military bases in the Middle East. The attacks follow rising tensions and stalled negotiations over Iran's nuclear program.

World.headline: Airspace closed, airlines halt flights as US, Israel attack, Iran responds
World.detail: Following a joint US-Israeli attack on Iran, Iranian forces launched missiles at several locations, including US military operations across the region, leading to airspace closures and flight halts. Explosions were reported in Tehran, Kermanshah, Qom, Tabriz, Isfahan, Ilam, and Karaj.

World.headline: Sweden confirms Russian drone intercepted near French aircraft carrier
World.detail: The Swedish military confirmed that a drone, which was jammed near a French aircraft carrier in the Oresund strait between Denmark and Sweden, was Russian. A Swedish navy vessel jammed the drone 13 kilometers (eight miles) from France's flagship, the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier.

World.headline: Bolivia cargo plane crash kills at least 15, scatters banknotes on the ground
World.detail: A military cargo plane carrying banknotes crashed during landing in Bolivia, killing at least 15 people. The plane crashed after veering off the runway and hitting an avenue, with police using tear gas to control crowds attempting to collect the scattered money.

World.headline: Pakistan declares 'open war' on Afghanistan as conflict sparks between neighbours
World.detail: Following intensifying border clashes, Pakistan has declared open war on Afghanistan. Tensions have risen, and Pakistan has bombed Kabul after cross-border attacks.

Zimbabwe.headline: Rising anger over 'lop-sided' and 'immoral' US health funding pacts with African countries
Zimbabwe.detail: There is increasing discontent regarding the perceived imbalances and ethical concerns associated with health funding agreements between the United States and various African nations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe's processing for white gold deepens with another sulphate plant
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is expanding its capacity to process lithium, often called 'white gold,' with the addition of a new sulphate plant.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mpilo Hospital seeks new Chief Medical Officer
Zimbabwe.detail: Mpilo Central Hospital is looking for a new Chief Medical Officer to lead its medical services.

Zimbabwe.headline: All set for tobacco selling season
Zimbabwe.detail: Preparations are complete for the upcoming tobacco selling season in Zimbabwe, a crucial period for the country's agricultural sector.

Zimbabwe.headline: Borrowdale residents clash with church over alleged fraudulent clearance letter
Zimbabwe.detail: Residents in Borrowdale are in conflict with a local church regarding a clearance letter that is suspected to be fraudulent.
